summary:
Only next-generation sequencing (RNA-seq) directly analyses intact RNA transcripts for gene expression, splicing variation, and mutation detection. Unlike PCR or Sanger sequencing, which can act on either DNA or RNA inputs respectively, RNA-seq is inherently structured around RNA-based molecular profiling.\boxed{C}

The summary focuses solely on clinically relevant facts extracted from the retrieved materials regarding RNA-specific diagnostic applications.
